name: Almond Wreath Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Country Living 12/95
In food processor with chopping blade, process almonds and 1/4 cup sugar until almonds are finely ground; set aside.
Lightly grease 5 baking sheets (or 2 baking sheets + 3 sheets of aluminum foil or parchment paper) to chill wreaths before baking.
In large bowl with mixer at medium speed, beat butter and remaining 1/2 cup sugar until light and fluffy.
Beat in egg, milk, vanilla, and almond extracts until well blended; reduce mixer speed to low.
Gradually beat in flour mixture (ground almonds, baking powder, and salt) until combined; add enough additional flour for soft dough to form.
Spoon about 1/3 of dough into a large pastry bag fitted with a 1/4-inch open star tip.
Pipe a 1/2-inch thick strip of dough onto baking sheets to form wreaths (about 2 inches in diameter), spacing them about 1 inch apart; refrigerate on baking sheets for 15 minutes to firm the dough.
Cut each green cherry into 8 slices; cut each red cherry into quarters.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Press a red cherry onto each wreath and place two green slices on each side of the red cherry.
Bake 8-10 minutes, until golden brown on top and lightly browned on bottom.
Cool completely on wire rack; store in an airtight container. Makes about 5 dozen cookies.
